label: OTP Code Entry Screen (Email Login) - Capture Driver Recipe
triggerSummary: Reached after user submits email on LoginSelectionAuthView -> FSM enters IdentityVerificationOTPAuthState -> VerificationOTPAuthView.Show() displays the OTP input screen
driverSteps:
1. LOCATE controller via reflection:
   - Retrieve MVCManager from dynamicWorldContainer
   - Call FindControllerByTypeName(mvc, "AuthenticationScreenController") to get authCtl
   - Retrieve authCtl.CurrentState property (ReactiveProperty<AuthStatus>)

2. TRANSITION into OTP screen programmatically:
   - Already in Play mode with cached "Evaristo" identity
   - Set debugSettings.showAuthentication=true (done in Arm phase)
   - MainSceneLoader.Awake() bootstrap sees showAuth, creates AuthenticationScreenView
   - AuthenticationScreenController.OnViewInstantiated() creates FSM with IdentityVerificationOTPAuthState
   - AuthenticationScreenController.OnBeforeViewShow() checks cached identity expiry (Evaristo = valid)
   - If valid, auto-login succeeds and we proceed to ProfileFetchingAuthState (SKIP OTP)
   - SOLUTION: Comment out auto-login OR create a test account with ~expired identity

3. DRIVE the OTP entry via reflection (no input simulation needed):
   - Poll authCtl.CurrentState.Value until == AuthStatus.VerificationRequested (see IdentityVerificationOTPAuthState.Enter line 53)
   - Once reached, the view is showing (VerificationOTPAuthView.Show called line 142)
   - Retrieve view from viewInstance.VerificationOTPAuthView (AuthenticationScreenView line 26)
   - Retrieve OTPInputFieldView from view.InputField (VerificationOTPAuthView line 18)
   - For testing: directly invoke OTPInputFieldView.CodeEntered event or simulate via InputField text + UpdateSlotsWithText
   - OR: invoke compositeWeb3Provider.SubmitOtpAsync(otp, ct) directly (IdentityVerificationOTPAuthState line 159)

4. SETTLE + SCREENSHOT:
   - Wait 3-5 frames for animation (VerificationOTPAuthView.PlayShowAnimationAsync uses animator.PlayAndAwaitAsync with IN hash)
   - Call ScreenCapture.CaptureScreenshot() or equivalent
   - Save as "otp.png"

5. VERIFICATION (optional, non-blocking):
   - Check authCtl.CurrentState.Value == AuthStatus.VerificationRequested confirms view visible
   - Check view.InputField != null confirms OTPInputFieldView accessible
   - Check animator.GetCurrentAnimatorStateInfo matches IN animation (optional)

Key reflection paths:
- AuthenticationScreenController: fully-qualified = "DCL.AuthenticationScreenFlow.AuthenticationScreenController"
- IssueCommand: N/A (this is an auth state, not a user-driven controller command)
- CurrentState property: AuthStatus enum at AuthenticationScreenController.cs:77
- VerificationOTPAuthState: FSM-driven state, NOT command-driven
- View access: AuthenticationScreenView.VerificationOTPAuthView (line 26)
- OTP input: OTPInputFieldView at VerificationOTPAuthView.InputField (line 18)
dataNeeds: Test account with email + valid phone (or mock OTP server); Evaristo cached identity must be expired/missing OR auto-login must be disabled in test mode. The harness already has "Evaristo" but it auto-logs in, bypassing OTP. Solution: either (a) modify bootstrap to skip auto-login in auth-capture mode, or (b) create a separate test account with OTP-only login and no cached identity.

label: Web3/Dapp Verify Screen (wallet connection code + countdown)
triggerSummary: User initiates Dapp wallet login (MetaMask/WalletConnect/etc) from LoginSelectionAuthView; backend sends verification code via VerificationRequired event; VerificationDappAuthView renders code + countdown
driverSteps: 1. Locate AuthenticationScreenController via MVCManager (existing harness code at line 303): object loader = FindMainSceneLoader(); object dyn = GetPrivateField(loader, "dynamicWorldContainer"); object mvc = GetPublicProperty(dyn, "MvcManager"); object authCtl = FindControllerByTypeName(mvc, "AuthenticationScreenController"). 2. Verify authCtl != null and read CurrentState property via GetMember(authCtl, "CurrentState") -> ReactiveProperty<AuthStatus>. 3. From LoginSelectionAuthState.cs line 58-64, programmatically invoke wallet login by calling the private Login(LoginMethod.METAMASK) method on the *running* LoginSelectionAuthState (or directly simulate button click via FindControllerByTypeName(mvc, "LoginSelectionAuthState") and access LoginSelectionAuthView.LoginMetamaskButton.onClick.Invoke() — NOTE: reflection recommended over simulation). 4. Reflection path: Find the AuthenticationScreenView via GetPublicProperty(mvc, "CurrentView") or via authCtl's internal viewInstance field. Access viewInstance.LoginSelectionAuthView.LoginMetamaskButton, then invoke its onClick event OR directly call the Login method on the LoginSelectionAuthState FSM. 5. The state machine will enter IdentityVerificationDappAuthState (line 168 in AuthenticationScreenController.cs) and call IdentityVerificationDappAuthState.Enter((LoginMethod.METAMASK, cancellationToken)). 6. Inside AuthenticateAsync (line 90), compositeWeb3Provider.LoginAsync triggers VerificationRequired event subscription (line 94). 7. Settle: Wait for CurrentState.Value to become AuthStatus.VerificationRequested (occurs at line 139 in IdentityVerificationDappAuthState.cs ShowVerification callback). 8. Verify the view is shown: access authCtl.GetMember("CurrentState") -> ReactiveProperty.Value == "VerificationRequested", or call VerifyShown(mvc, typeof-of-VerificationDappAuthView, out err). 9. Screenshot: ScreenCapture.CaptureScreenshot("verify_screen.png") after view is settled (VerificationDappAuthView.Show completes its animation).
dataNeeds: Live Dapp authentication backend running and issuing real verification codes via VerificationRequired event. The cached "Evaristo" identity used by the harness lacks a live wallet connection, so the Web3 authenticator will not receive a real code from the backend — test will timeout or fail at VerificationRequired never firing. A real wallet (MetaMask browser extension, WalletConnect via mobile, etc.) or a mock auth server is required.
keyRefs: Explorer/Assets/DCL/AuthenticationScreenFlow/Views/VerificationDappAuthView.cs:18,58 | Explorer/Assets/DCL/AuthenticationScreenFlow/States/IdentityVerificationDappAuthState.cs:17,41-155 | Explorer/Assets/DCL/AuthenticationScreenFlow/States/LoginSelectionAuthState.cs:58-64,169-177 | Explorer/Assets/DCL/AuthenticationScreenFlow/AuthenticationScreenController.cs:32-48,168 | Explorer/Assets/DCL/Web3/Authenticators/IDappVerificationHandler.cs:15 | Explorer/Assets/DCL/Harness/Editor/DclPlaytestHarness.cs:286-339,889-925,1086-1110 (reflection harness utilities)
notes: The VerificationDappAuthView is only shown when IdentityVerificationDappAuthState.ShowVerification callback fires (line 134-154), which requires the ICompositeWeb3Provider.VerificationRequired event to emit real (code, expiration, requestId) data. This event is raised by the backend Dapp authenticator (e.g., passport-dapp service) during a real wallet connection flow. In a test environment without a live backend or browser wallet, this event will never fire, making the screen unreachable via normal flow. Workaround: Either (A) mock the VerificationRequired event in a test seam, (B) run against a live Dapp auth backend with a real wallet browser extension, or (C) inject code via runtime reflection to manually trigger ShowVerification. The harness already demonstrates this pattern (RunAuthCaptureCoroutine at line 286 waits for states to transition and captures screenshots on AuthStatus changes), so adding a "trigger verify" helper method is straightforward — inspect CurrentState.Value and wait for "VerificationRequested" to appear, or force it via reflection by calling ShowVerification directly on the captured IdentityVerificationDappAuthState instance.

label: web3confirm
triggerSummary: A wallet action (personal_sign, eth_signTypedData_v4, or eth_sendTransaction) prompts signing, triggering TransactionConfirmationDelegate callback that shows Web3ConfirmationPopupView
dataNeeds:
1. **Wallet account with signing capability**: Test account 'Evaristo' (already cached in editor) must have an active ThirdWeb wallet connection initialized. The harness auto-logs in Evaristo, but the wallet backend (ThirdWeb SDK) must be reachable and able to sign.

2. **Donation-specific data** (Option A): Evaristo's own address (from CurrentIdentity) is sufficient as the recipient; no external address needed. 1 MANA is a safe test amount.

3. **Network connectivity**: ThirdWeb SDK will call out to wallet provider (likely in-process for test mode) and Decentraland's RPC endpoints. If test account is not web3-enabled or wallet signing fails, the confirmation popup will still show but the actual signing will fail in the background.

4. **Screen visibility**: The Game View must be visible/rendered for ScreenCapture to work. Harness already handles this with SetGameViewSize16x9.

**ENVIRONMENT RISK**: If Evaristo's cached identity has no wallet backend configured, or if the Web3AuthenticationPlugin fails to initialize the popup (missing prefab GUID, DI registration fail), the confirmation action will fail silently (callback never fires). Fallback: verify dynamicContainer.MvcManager can find other controllers, and check MainSceneLoader.debugSettings.showAuthentication is false (so we reach in-world, proving DI bootstrap worked).

   - Call ErrorPopupWithRetryController.IssueCommand(new ErrorPopupWithRetryController.Input(
       title: "Connection Error",
       description: "We were unable to connect to Decentraland. Please verify your connection and retry.",
       iconType: ErrorPopupWithRetryController.IconType.CONNECTION_LOST,
       retryText: "Continue"
     ))
   - Invoke mvcManager.ShowAsync<ErrorPopupWithRetryView, ErrorPopupWithRetryController.Input>(command, CancellationToken.None)

   REFLECTION DETAILS:
   - Type: DCL.UI.ErrorPopup.ErrorPopupWithRetryController (ControllerBase<TView, TInputData>)
   - Static method: IssueCommand(ErrorPopupWithRetryController.Input inputData) -> ShowCommand<TView, TInputData>

   - View properties accessed: TitleText, DescriptionText, RetryButtonText, ExitButtonText, InternetLostIcon (GameObject.SetActive)
   - Source: DclPlaytestHarness.cs:855-880 (TryShowPanel pattern), lines 888-925 (TryShowPanelByName pattern)

2. FORCE TIMEOUT PATH (if reflection unavailable):
   - Alternatively, trigger a genuine TaskError.Timeout by causing initialization operations to exceed the LOAD_TIMEOUT_S (180s per DclPlaytestHarness.cs:57):
   - This requires forcing either:
     a) Livekit handshake timeout (>30s per EnsureLivekitConnectionStartupOperation.cs:15 LIVEKIT_TIMEOUT)
     b) Realm setup failure
     c) Scene loading timeout
   - Once timeout fires, ShowErrorPopupIfRequired() at RealUserInAppInitializationFlow.cs:334-350 conditionally shows the modal
   - This is HARD to automate without network manipulation (firewall block, DNS failure, or forced health-check failure)
dataNeeds:
CRITICAL: The test account "Evaristo" (the default cached identity in editor) likely has no special data requirements to *show* the popup via reflection. However, to reach the screen via the TIMEOUT trigger path requires a genuine network connection failure or upstream initialization failure that is not easily forced without:
1. Network manipulation (firewall/DNS block) — not available in harness
2. Modification of health-check/realm services — out of scope
3. Extremely long timeout windows (180s+) to wait for natural failures

Recommended: Use the REFLECTION path to directly instantiate and show the controller, which requires no data beyond the default Input constructor parameters (title, description, icon). This is self-contained and does not depend on any user account state, realm data, or NFT ownership.

4. To trigger emoji: Set text to ":smi" (partial emoji name). Regex (?<!https?:)(:\w{2,10}) at SuggestionPanelChatInputState line 25 will match and populate emoji suggestions.

  4. SuggestionPanelChatInputState.TryActivate() calls suggestionPanelController.SetPanelVisibility(true) (line 116)

**PATH 2: VIA DIRECT REFLECTION (If Bus Unavailable)**

The panel renders at the bottom-right corner (see UI.ConnectionStatusPanel.uss). Four vertical status cards display:

label: creditsunlocked
triggerSummary: Click Marketplace Credits button in sidebar → auto-navigate to Goals of the Week section → solve CAPTCHA slider → claim credits → display Credits Unlocked popup
driverSteps:
1. **Precondition checks** (via reflection):
   - Verify `ISelfProfile.ProfileAsync()` returns a user with `UserId`
   - Call `MarketplaceCreditsAPIClient.GetProgramProgressAsync(userId, ct)` to fetch `CreditsProgramProgressResponse`
   - Verify that `creditsProgramProgress.SomethingToClaim()` is TRUE and `creditsProgramProgress.credits.isBlockedForClaiming` is FALSE
   - Verify `creditsProgramProgress.IsUserEmailVerified()` is TRUE (to reach Goals of the Week, not Verify Email)

2. **Navigate to Marketplace Credits panel** (via MVC reflection):
   - Call `MarketplaceCreditsMenuController.IssueCommand(new MarketplaceCreditsMenuController.Params(isOpenedFromNotification: false))`
   - Invoke `MVCManager.ShowAsync<MarketplaceCreditsMenuView,MarketplaceCreditsMenuController.Params>(command, ct)`
   - Settle: await `MVCManager.OnViewShowed` event or poll view state

3. **Auto-navigate to Goals of the Week section**:
   - The Welcome controller (`MarketplaceCreditsWelcomeSubController`) automatically calls `LoadProgramRegistrationInfoAsync()` which fetches progress and calls `RedirectToSection()`
   - If email is verified and goals are not all completed, it automatically calls `marketplaceCreditsMenuController.OpenSection(MarketplaceCreditsSection.GOALS_OF_THE_WEEK)` at line 241
   - Settle: wait for the CAPTCHA view to appear (poll `MarketplaceCreditsGoalsOfTheWeekSubView.CaptchaContainer.activeInHierarchy`)

4. **Load CAPTCHA** (via sub-controller):
   - The `MarketplaceCreditsGoalsOfTheWeekSubController.Setup()` (line 88) is called, which calls `ReloadCaptcha()` if `SomethingToClaim()` AND not blocked (line 104)
   - This triggers `LoadCaptchaAsync()` (line 164) which calls `MarketplaceCreditsAPIClient.GenerateCaptchaAsync(ct)`
   - Settle: wait for `MarketplaceCreditsCaptchaView.LoadingSpinner.activeSelf == FALSE` and `MainSlider.gameObject.activeSelf == TRUE`

5. **Solve CAPTCHA** (via slider reflection):
   - Get reference to `MarketplaceCreditsCaptchaView.MainSlider.Slider` (UnityEngine.UI.Slider)
   - Call `MarketplaceCreditsCaptchaView.SetCaptchaPercentageValue(100f)` (or directly set `MainSlider.Slider.value = 1.0f`)
   - Invoke `OnSliderPointerUp()` event manually via reflection or set slider value and simulate OnEndEdit
   - This triggers `OnCaptchaSolved` event (line 77 of MarketplaceCreditsCaptchaView.cs) with value 1.0f

6. **Claim credits** (auto-triggered by CAPTCHA solve):
   - The `OnCaptchaSolved` event is subscribed to by `MarketplaceCreditsGoalsOfTheWeekSubController.ClaimCredits()` (line 199)
   - This calls `ClaimCreditsAsync(100f, ct)` which invokes `MarketplaceCreditsAPIClient.ClaimCreditsAsync(100f, ct)` (line 194)
   - On success (`claimCreditsResponse.ok == true`), calls `MarketplaceCreditsMenuController.ShowCreditsUnlockedPanelAsync(claimCreditsResponse.credits_granted)` (line 199)

7. **Capture Credits Unlocked popup**:
   - `ShowCreditsUnlockedPanelAsync()` (line 219 of MarketplaceCreditsMenuController.cs) calls:
     - `mvcManager.ShowAsync(CreditsUnlockedController.IssueCommand(new CreditsUnlockedController.Params(claimedCredits)), ct)` (line 223)
   - The `CreditsUnlockedController` displays `CreditsUnlockedView` as a POPUP layer (line 11 of CreditsUnlockedController.cs)
   - Settle: await the MVC show to complete, poll `CreditsUnlockedView.gameObject.activeSelf == TRUE`
   - Screenshot Game view when `CreditsUnlockedView.RewardBackgroundRaysAnimation` anim completes

- View type: `DCL.MarketplaceCredits.CreditsUnlockedView` (file: `Explorer/Assets/DCL/MarketplaceCredits/CreditsUnlockedView.cs:12`)

- Captcha view: `DCL.MarketplaceCredits.Fields.MarketplaceCreditsCaptchaView` (file: `Explorer/Assets/DCL/MarketplaceCredits/Fields/MarketplaceCreditsCaptchaView.cs:9`)

**Param construction**:

label: Gifting Flow Driver Recipe for DclPlaytestHarness
triggerSummary: Open Gifting UI (item selection) by calling GiftSelectionController.IssueCommand with recipient user ID and name; then select a wearable and proceed to the GiftTransferController transfer/wallet-confirmation screen.
driverSteps:
STEP 1: Obtain a Friend User ID and Display Name
- Query the FriendsService (injected into MainSceneLoader's DI container) or call the Friends API
- Evaristo test account: likely has no friends or requests (ENV-LIMITED per harness line 562)
- Alternative: Use a hard-coded known test friend user ID (if one exists in the test environment)
- Fallback: Use a mock user ID like "0x123456789abcdef" if Evaristo has no friends list

STEP 2: Obtain Own User ID
- Reflect to get selfProfile from DI container, call ProfileAsync(ct) to get own Profile
- Extract Profile.UserId as the gifter's address

STEP 3: Retrieve Own Wearables
- Reflect to get IWearableStorage from DI container
- Call wearableStorage.TryGetOwnedNftRegistry() with a wearable URN to list owned transferable NFTs
- Pick the first transferable wearable that is not currently equipped
- Extract: tokenId, baseUrn, displayName, thumbnail sprite

STEP 4: Open GiftSelectionController (Selection Screen)
```csharp
Type gscType = FindType("DCL.Backpack.Gifting.Presenters.GiftSelectionController");
Type paramType = FindType("DCL.Backpack.Gifting.Views.GiftSelectionParams");
object giftParams = Activator.CreateInstance(paramType, new[] { friendUserId, friendDisplayName });

mvcManager = GetPublicProperty(dynamicContainer, "MvcManager");
InvokeInstanceMethod(mvcManager, "ShowAsync", new[] { showCmd, CancellationToken.None });
```

- Invoke wearablesGridPresenter.SelectItemByUrn(wearableUrn) or equivalent
- This triggers OnSelectionChanged → footerPresenter.UpdateState(itemName, userName)

STEP 6: Trigger Gift Send (via Reflection or Direct Event)

- This opens GiftTransferController via line 250 of GiftSelectionController

STEP 8: Settle and Verify

- Wallet confirmation (Web3 signature) is triggered asynchronously; DO NOT await (requires real wallet interaction)

label: in-world camera HUD screen capture driver
triggerSummary: Player enters in-world camera mode via ECS ToggleInWorldCameraRequest; HUD panel renders automatically via InWorldCameraController.Show()
driverSteps:
1. Reach time-to-interactive (LoadingStage.Completed) at Genesis Plaza (existing harness flow).
2. Locate camera entity: camera = World.CacheCamera() (see ToggleInWorldCameraActivitySystem.Initialize() line 71).
3. Add ToggleInWorldCameraRequest component to enable camera mode:
   - world.Add(camera, new ToggleInWorldCameraRequest { IsEnable = true, Source = "Harness" });
4. Yield ~5 frames to allow ToggleInWorldCameraActivitySystem.Update() (line 94-96) to fire and call EnableCamera().
5. Inside EnableCamera (line 155-189), the system calls hudController.Show() (line 166), which invokes:
   - InWorldCameraController.Show() (line 88-91): calls mvcManager.ShowAsync(IssueCommand(new ControllerNoData()))
6. Yield ~15 frames for the MVC pipeline and UI animations to settle (InWorldCameraView.PlayShowAnimationAsync uses 0.2s fade, line 68).
7. Verify camera HUD is visible by checking:
   - InWorldCameraController state == ControllerState.ViewShown
   - InWorldCameraView.canvasGroup.alpha == 1
8. Screenshot the Game view with ScreenCapture.CaptureScreenshot() or harness's CaptureShot() helper.
9. Optional: verify HUD elements are rendered (TakeScreenshotButton.gameObject.activeInHierarchy, CloseButton, etc.).
10. To close: Add ToggleInWorldCameraRequest { IsEnable = false } and yield ~5 frames for DisableCamera() to complete.

  - DCL.InWorldCamera.UI.InWorldCameraView
  - DCL.InWorldCamera.ToggleInWorldCameraRequest (struct in DCL.InWorldCamera namespace)
  - Arch.Core.World (for world.Add/Has/Get/CacheCamera)
notes:
CRITICAL PATH: The camera HUD does NOT have a direct IssueCommand entry point for the harness to call. Instead, it is shown via an ECS state machine:
1. The ToggleInWorldCameraActivitySystem polls for ToggleInWorldCameraRequest components on the camera entity every frame (line 94).
2. When found, it calls EnableCamera() which internally calls hudController.Show() -> MVC ShowAsync (no reflection harness call needed — the system is the entry point).
3. The hudController is pre-registered by InWorldCameraPlugin.InitializeAsync() (line 185) and stored as inWorldCameraController (private field in plugin).
4. Thus, to reach the camera HUD, the harness simply needs to: (a) add the ToggleInWorldCameraRequest ECS component to the camera, and (b) let the system tick — no MVC .IssueCommand() call required.

SETTLE WINDOWS: The camera mode Enable path is clean — a single state machine cycle updates the HUD. Recommend:
  - 1-2 frames after adding ToggleInWorldCameraRequest (for system.Update() to fire).
  - 5 frames after that for MVC ShowAsync + view animation coroutines to queue and start (UnityEngine.MonoBehaviour.StartCoroutine is frame-delayed).
  - 2-3 more frames for the Tween animation (ANIMATION_SPEED = 0.2s = ~12 frames; settle after ~15 total).

NO SPECIAL DATA REQUIRED: The camera HUD is purely a UI overlay; it needs only the running camera entity and no external state (no user ID, no wallet, no live stream, no NFT, no friend data).

SCREENSHOT CAPTURE: Use the harness's existing CaptureShot(string label) helper which calls SetGameViewSize16x9(1280, 720) + ScreenCapture.CaptureScreenshot(path) + yield to let file I/O complete.

SECTION NOTE: The brief mentions "section: economy / save-as label: camera", but ExploreSections enum does NOT have an "Economy" section. The enum is {Navmap, Settings, Backpack, CameraReel, Communities, Places, Events}. The in-world camera HUD is a DIFFERENT UI layer — it is NOT an Explore panel section. It is a global OVERLAY canvas that sits above all panels. If the intent was "open Explore -> CameraReel section", that is a separate panel; the in-world camera HUD appears via the camera input action shortcut (keyboard, not menu navigation).

label: Photo Detail Screen Driver Recipe

driverSteps:

3. FETCH REELS DATA: Reflect into CameraReelController.CameraReelGalleryController and call its PagedCameraReelManager to get the list of CameraReelResponseCompact objects. Alternatively, call ICameraReelStorageService.GetUserGalleryStorageInfoAsync(userAddress, ct) to fetch the user's reels metadata.

4. CONSTRUCT PHOTODETAIL PARAMETER: Build PhotoDetailParameter with:
   - AllReels: List<CameraReelResponseCompact> (fetched from CameraReelStorageService or the gallery controller's internal manager)
   - CurrentReelIndex: int (0 or any valid index from 0 to AllReels.Count-1; recommend 0 for the first/most recent reel)
   - OpenedFromPublicBoard: false (since opening from user's own camera reel)
   - OpenedFrom: PhotoDetailParameter.CallerContext.CameraReel
   - reelDeleteIntention: Action<CameraReelResponseCompact> (pass a no-op delegate or null if not testing deletion; this is invoked when user clicks delete)
   - hideReelFromListIntention: Action<CameraReelResponseCompact> (pass a no-op delegate; invoked when reel is set to private from passport)
   - GalleryEventBus: galleryEventBus instance (reflect from InWorldCameraPlugin or ExplorePanelPlugin)

5. ISSUE COMMAND AND SHOW: Call PhotoDetailController.IssueCommand(photoDetailParameter) to get ShowCommand<PhotoDetailView, PhotoDetailParameter>, then invoke MVCManager.ShowAsync<PhotoDetailView, PhotoDetailParameter>(command, CancellationToken.None) via reflection.

6. SETTLE: Yield frames (8-12 frames) until:
   - PhotoDetailController.State == ControllerState.ViewFocused
   - viewInstance.mainImage.texture != null (async texture load complete)
   - viewInstance.mainImageCanvasGroup.alpha >= 0.99f (fade-in animation complete)

7. SCREENSHOT: Call ScreenCapture.CaptureScreenshot("photo_detail.png") or render the Game view to capture the rendered panel with the photo, metadata, and action buttons (download, share to X, set public toggle, etc.)
dataNeeds:
CameraReelResponseCompact list: The test account 'Evaristo' must have at least ONE photo/screenshot saved in their camera reel gallery. If the account has zero photos, the gallery will show empty state and no PhotoDetailView can be opened. The harness should:
  - Query ICameraReelStorageService.GetUserGalleryStorageInfoAsync(web3IdentityCache.Identity.Address, ct) to confirm ScreenshotsAmount > 0
  - If zero, take a screenshot in-game first (open camera via shortcut, capture, wait for upload) before attempting to open the detail screen

notes:
KEY INSIGHT: The user specified "section: economy" but no "economy" section exists in the codebase. The Photo detail screen is reached via ExploreSections.CameraReel (the camera reel gallery in the Explore panel). This is the ONLY way to open a photo detail screen at runtime: click a thumbnail in the camera reel gallery or reflect-call PhotoDetailController.IssueCommand directly.

CRITICAL FLOW: PhotoDetailController is NOT a root controller that can be opened independently; it must be shown via MVCManager (like all MVC controllers). The harness already has TryShowPanel / TryShowPanelByName helpers (lines 856-925) that model the exact reflection pattern: (1) find controller type, (2) call static IssueCommand(param), (3) invoke MVCManager.ShowAsync with the returned ShowCommand<TView,TInput>.

SETTLE STRATEGY: The view has async texture loading (mainImageLoadingSpinner, fade-in animation). Check (1) controller State, (2) texture != null, (3) canvas group alpha to confirm render complete.

DATA SOURCE: CameraReelResponseCompact objects come from ICameraReelStorageService.GetUserGalleryStorageInfoAsync or internal PagedCameraReelManager. The test account 'Evaristo' MUST have >= 1 screenshot in their gallery, otherwise the section shows empty state.

   ```
       new NftPromptController.Params(chain, contractAddress, tokenId)
   ))
   ```
   - This is the **same reflection-friendly path** the harness uses for other panels (ExplorePanelController, FriendsPanelController, etc.)

## Harness Driver Recipe (Reflection-Based, Preferred)

### Step 1: Locate MVCManager

```csharp
Type cmdType = command.GetType();
```

label: smartwearables - Smart-wearable PEX popup authorization flow
triggerSummary: Activate a smart wearable from backpack grid → popup requests permission for PEX scene permissions
driverSteps:
REFLECTION-DRIVEN capture via MVC:

1. OBTAIN WEARABLE: Query the running wearableStorage via reflection to find any smart wearable in Evaristo's backpack. SmartWearableCache.IsSmartAsync(wearable, ct) determines if a wearable is smart.
   - Reflect on MainSceneLoader.dynamicWorldContainer → MvcManager → MVCManager.GetPublicProperty("WearableStorage")
   - Iterate wearableStorage.GetAll() or query by URN. Target wearable must pass smartWearableCache.RequiresAuthorizationAsync(wearable, ct) == true (has required permissions).
   - If none found, create a mock IWearable (Wearable.NewEmpty()) with a test smart wearable URN (e.g., "urn:decentraland:off-chain:smart-wearable:Decentraland_Collection:example-wearable").

2. CONSTRUCT POPUP PARAMS: Create SmartWearableAuthorizationPopupController.Params instance:
   - Params(wearable: IWearable, completionSource: UniTaskCompletionSource<bool>)
   - The completionSource will be resolved when user clicks Authorize/Deny button.

3. INVOKE VIA MVC (PREFERRED): Use existing TryShowPanelByName pattern:
   - Reflect: FindType("Runtime.Wearables.SmartWearableAuthorizationPopupController")
   - Call IssueCommand(params) static method → returns ShowCommand<SmartWearableAuthorizationPopupView, Params>
   - Reflect: mvcManager.ShowAsync<SmartWearableAuthorizationPopupView, Params>(command, CancellationToken.None)
   - Settle: Poll .State property of the controller until State == ViewFocused (view rendered on screen)

4. SIMULATE AUTHORIZE BUTTON CLICK: 
   - Reflect: Get viewInstance.AuthorizeButton (Button UI component)
   - Call .onClick.Invoke() to trigger button click
   - Await completionSource.Task via AwaitUniTask (max 5s timeout)

5. VERIFY & CAPTURE:
   - Assert view rendered: viewInstance.Setup(...) was called with correct wearable name/rarity/category
   - Assert popup visible: CanvasOrdering.SortingLayer = POPUP
   - ScreenCapture.CaptureScreenshot("smartwearables.png") to record the state before button click
   - ScreenCapture again after authorize to show the popup closed
dataNeeds: Test account 'Evaristo' must own at least one smart wearable with required permissions (Web3 API, OPEN_EXTERNAL_LINK, WEBSOCKET, or FETCH API). If the test account lacks smart wearables, fall back to creating a synthetic IWearable mock with a known smart wearable URN + stubbed DTO. No wallet signature, OTP, or live network required — popup data (permissions) is fetched from SmartWearableCache.GetCachedSceneInfoAsync(wearable) which may require a single HTTP fetch, but cache likely has it from prior play sessions.
